name: CIB seven - SOAP Connector
description:
Call a SOAP web service from a BPMN Service Task using the CIB seven SOAP connector.

HOW TO USE (Camunda 7): apply to a bpmn:ServiceTask. The task's bpmn:extensionElements must contain a <camunda:connector> holding <camunda:connectorId>soap-http-connector</camunda:connectorId> and a <camunda:inputOutput>. The HTTP method is always POST (the connector rejects anything else).

INPUTS (camunda:inputParameter, by name): 'method' = POST (fixed); 'url' = absolute SOAP endpoint URL, required; 'payload' = the full SOAP envelope XML to send as the body, required (wrap in <![CDATA[ ... ]]> in BPMN); 'headers' = a Map<String,String>, REQUIRED because the connector sets no defaults - it must include Content-Type and, for SOAP 1.1, SOAPAction; 'request-config' = an optional Map of HTTP-client options.

OUTPUTS (camunda:outputParameter; the source expression reads the connector response): name=<your var> source=${statusCode} (Integer), source=${response} (response XML String), source=${headers} (response headers Map).

MAPS: this engine uses EL 2.2 which CANNOT evaluate inline map literals - do NOT write ${ {...} }. Provide 'headers' and 'request-config' either as a <camunda:map> with <camunda:entry key="...">value</camunda:entry> children inside the inputParameter, or as ${someVar} referencing a process variable that already holds a java.util.Map. For SOAP 1.1 use Content-Type=text/xml; charset=utf-8 plus a SOAPAction header; for SOAP 1.2 use Content-Type=application/soap+xml.

COMPLETE EXAMPLE (service task body): <camunda:connector><camunda:connectorId>soap-http-connector</camunda:connectorId><camunda:inputOutput><camunda:inputParameter name="method">POST</camunda:inputParameter><camunda:inputParameter name="url">http://www.dneonline.com/calculator.asmx</camunda:inputParameter><camunda:inputParameter name="payload"><![CDATA[<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"><soap:Body><Add xmlns="http://tempuri.org/"><intA>3</intA><intB>4</intB></Add></soap:Body></soap:Envelope>]]></camunda:inputParameter><camunda:inputParameter name="headers"><camunda:map><camunda:entry key="Content-Type">text/xml; charset=utf-8</camunda:entry><camunda:entry key="SOAPAction">http://tempuri.org/Add</camunda:entry></camunda:map></camunda:inputParameter><camunda:outputParameter name="statusCode">${statusCode}</camunda:outputParameter><camunda:outputParameter name="response">${response}</camunda:outputParameter></camunda:inputOutput></camunda:connector>

properties.label: Endpoint URL
properties.description: Absolute SOAP endpoint URL, e.g. https://service.example.com/OrderService. Expressions are allowed, e.g. ${endpoint}.

properties.label: SOAP envelope
properties.description: The full SOAP envelope (XML) to send as the request body. A String or an expression, e.g. ${envelope}.

properties.label: Headers (map)
properties.description: SOAP request headers as a Map<String,String>; required (the connector sets no defaults). The engine (EL 2.2) does NOT support inline map literals, so do not use ${ {...} }. Supply the map either as ${headersVar} referencing a Map process variable, or - when emitting BPMN directly - as a <camunda:map> with <camunda:entry> children. For SOAP 1.1 set Content-Type=text/xml; charset=utf-8 and the SOAPAction header; for SOAP 1.2 use Content-Type=application/soap+xml. SOAPAction is itself a header, not a separate input parameter.

properties.label: Request config (map)
properties.description: Optional request-config options as a Map. Inline map literals (${ {...} }) are NOT supported by the engine; supply ${configVar} (a Map process variable) or a <camunda:map> of <camunda:entry> children in the BPMN. Supported keys: authentication-enabled, circular-redirects-allowed, connection-timeout, connection-keep-alive, connection-request-timeout, content-compression-enabled, cookie-spec, expect-continue-enabled, hard-cancellation-enabled, max-redirects, proxy, proxy-preferred-auth-scheme, redirects-enabled, response-timeout, target-preferred-auth-schemes.

properties.label: Status code -> variable
properties.description: Process variable that receives the HTTP status code.

properties.label: Response body -> variable
properties.description: Process variable that receives the SOAP response XML.

properties.label: Response headers -> variable
properties.description: Process variable that receives the response headers map.
